article_title: Meta Delays Avocado AI Model to May After Internal Testing Reveals Performance Gaps with Google, OpenAI and Anthropic
summary: Article contains a fabricated claim about Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang omitting Llama from an earnings call, which contradicts publicly available earnings transcripts. NYT source URL needs www prefix. Otherwise well-structured and factually sound.

content_preview.title: Meta Delays Avocado AI Model to May After Internal Testing Reveals Performance Gaps with Google, OpenAI and Anthropic
content_preview.summary: Meta postpones its next-generation Avocado AI model by at least two months after benchmarks show it trailing rivals, raising questions about the company's $135 billion AI investment.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Meta has pushed the release of its next-generation AI model, codenamed Avocado, from March to at least May 2026 after internal testing revealed it trails the latest systems from Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic in key capability areas, according to [The New York Times](https://nytimes.com/2026/03/12/technology/meta-avocado-ai-model-delay.html). The delay puts fresh scrutiny on the company's plan to spend between $115 billion and $135 billion on AI infrastructure this year.

## What We ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) returned dead link errors — verify URLs before resubmitting
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.source_verification: 1. NYT (nytimes.com/2026/03/12/...): URL returns 404 without www. prefix. Article exists at www.nytimes.com and is widely cited across dozens of outlets. VERIFIED — URL format needs correction. 2. Fortune (fortune.com/2026/03/13/...): Accessible and verified. Confirms Gemini licensing discussion, Avocado delay, $14.3B Alexandr Wang deal. 3. U.S. News/Reuters (money.usnews.com/...): Domain not on allowlist. Bot-blocked (403) but story confirmed via web search. Reuters wire story confirms spokesperson quote, benchmark comparisons, delay timeline.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: CRITICAL — Fabricated claim: The Analysis section states Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang 'reportedly omitted Llama from his list of major customer deployments during a recent earnings call.' This claim is not attributed to any cited source and contradicts Nvidia's Q4 FY2026 earnings call (Feb 25, 2026), which highlighted a 'multiyear, multigenerational strategic partnership with Meta' and mentioned Llama Nemotron. Must be removed. MINOR — The Megan Fu quote appears to originate from The Information, not from the three cited sources. All other claims verified: Avocado delay March-to-May, Gemini 2.5/3.0 comparisons, Gemini 3.0 release Nov 18 2025, Meta spokesperson quote via Reuters, Gemini licensing discussion via Fortune/NYT, capex $115-135B, LeCun departure Nov 2025 (AMI Labs), Wang $14.3B deal via CNBC, 600 positions eliminated Oct 2025.
editor_notes.content_quality: Structure is strong (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis). Tone is neutral and measured. Word count (567) is within News range (400-1200). No AI self-reference. Article effectively contextualizes the delay within Meta's broader AI strategy challenges.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and measured throughout. No sensationalism. Appropriate hedging on unconfirmed details (licensing discussion described as 'discussed' with 'no decision confirmed').
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage found in existing articles. Related coverage exists on Yann LeCun's AMI Labs but no overlap on the Avocado delay story.
concerns: Jensen Huang/Nvidia claim in Analysis section is unsourced and contradicts public earnings transcripts — appears fabricated
NYT source URL missing www. prefix causes 404
Megan Fu quote attribution gap — source appears to be The Information, not cited
recommendations: Remove or replace the fabricated Jensen Huang/Nvidia claim with a verifiable statement
Either add The Information as a source for the Megan Fu quote or rephrase attribution
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REQUEST_CHANGES. The article is well-structured and mostly factually accurate, but contains one critical fabricated claim about Jensen Huang omitting Llama from Nvidia earnings that must be removed before publication. The NYT URL also needs a www. prefix fix. Once these issues are addressed, the article would be suitable for publication.
